front: How do phospholipase A2 (PLA2) snake venom enzymes cause toxicity?
back: PLA2 enzymes hydrolyze the sn-2 position of membrane phospholipids, releasing arachidonic acid (pro-inflammatory) and lysophospholipids (detergent-like). Effects: myotoxicity, neurotoxicity, hemolysis, inflammation. Some PLA2s are catalytically inactive but retain toxicity through receptor binding.
